task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ/Fiji interface showing an open image window titled 'Fluorescent Cells' or 'Fluorescent Cells (400K)'.

task_completion.description: Split the multi-channel image into separate Red and Green channels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Separate image windows visible for the individual channels, typically named 'C1-Fluorescent Cells' (Red) and 'C2-Fluorescent Cells' (Green).

task_completion.description: Define the cell area by creating a mask from the Green channel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A binary (black and white) or thresholded image window derived from the Green channel, or a Threshold tool dialog actively being applied to the Green channel.

task_completion.description: Generate a 32-bit floating-point ratio image (Red / Green) restricted to the mask.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Image Calculator dialog showing a 'Divide' operation between the Red and Green channels with '32-bit (float) result' checked, and/or a resulting 32-bit image window where background pixels are 0 or NaN.

task_completion.description: Measure the mean intensity of the ratio image within the cell mask.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ 'Results' window displaying a measurement row with a 'Mean' value calculated from the ratio image.

task_completion.description: Save the ratio image to 'ratio_map.tif' and the mean intensity to 'mean_ratio.txt'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Save dialogs, file manager, or terminal windows showing 'ratio_map.tif' and 'mean_ratio.txt' being saved or existing in the '~/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the ImageJ/Fiji graphical interface to perform the image processing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with ImageJ menus, toolbars, and dialogs rather than just running a pre-written script in a terminal.

integrity.description: The ratio image was actually computed using image math.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of the Image Calculator dialog or macro execution showing division, rather than just renaming or copying an existing file.

integrity.description: The mean intensity was genuinely measured from the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Results' table in ImageJ is populated with measurement data, rather than the agent manually typing a guessed number into a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using the built-in Fiji/ImageJ sample 'Fluorescent Cells (400K)', accessible via File > Open Samples. The sample is a multi-channel image containing Red and Green channels. The expected outputs are a 32-bit TIFF image saved at '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/ratio_map.tif' and a text file at '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/mean_ratio.txt'. The ratio must be Red divided by Green, masked by the Green channel.
